articles.headline: SMS Declaration of Compliance
articles.blurb: What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.
articles.takeaway: Operators must formally document their safety management system compliance to demonstrate to regulators that they have implemented required safety processes and controls.

articles.headline: 5 Aircraft Used By The US Military That Are Larger Than Commercial Jets
articles.blurb: The largest USAF aircraft are converted Boeing commercial aircraft, although it also operates massive purpose-built bomber and transport aircraft.
articles.takeaway: Military variants of commercial airframes often exceed their civilian counterparts in size and capability through significant structural modifications and purpose-built designs.

articles.headline: Why Faster Aircraft Don’t Always Save Time
articles.blurb: Many other factors can also affect journey length.
articles.takeaway: Ground operations, weather delays, and routing constraints often eliminate time savings from aircraft speed, making operational efficiency more critical than airframe performance.

articles.headline: Is It True That The Airbus A350’s Price Tag Is 50% Less Than The Boeing 787’s?

articles.takeaway: The A350's lower price reflects Airbus's focus on optimizing proven technologies rather than pursuing cutting-edge innovations like the 787's composite-heavy design.

articles.headline: NASA stops work on SLS Mobile Launcher 2
articles.blurb: NASA has stopped work on a second mobile launch platform intended for an upgraded version of the Space Launch System the agency no longer plans to develop.
articles.takeaway: NASA's decision to halt ML2 development signals a strategic shift toward focusing resources on the core SLS program rather than maintaining infrastructure for a never-materialized upgraded variant.

articles.headline: Crossing the ‘Line of Death’ at 2,125 MPH: SR-71 Battle Damage Assessment after ‘El Dorado Canyon’
articles.blurb: In the aftermath of Operation El Dorado Canyon, the Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird was sent across Qaddafi’s ‘Line of Death’ for a high-risk overflight of Libya to deliver critical battle damage assessment. After a joint strike mission conducted by United States Air Force (USAF) and United States Navy...
articles.takeaway: The SR-71's post-strike reconnaissance role demonstrated that hypersonic aircraft capabilities remained operationally irreplaceable for rapid damage assessment in hostile airspace despite surface-to-air threats.

articles.headline: Thinking of You, Earth

The Artemis II astronauts – Wiseman and fellow NASA astronauts Christina Koch and Victor Glover, and CSA...
articles.takeaway: Artemis II's 2026 lunar mission demonstrates NASA's commitment to sustained deep-space human spaceflight, establishing the operational cadence needed for eventual Mars exploration.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Integrated operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flight management offers the most immediate pathway to sustainability gains while accommodating projected traffic growth.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby

articles.takeaway: Artemis 2 astronauts will conduct real-time lunar observations during flyby, enabling scientists to validate instrument capabilities and refine landing site selection for future crewed surface missions.
